transcript: 藢t蕦伞 蓹v 藞w蓴藧r
definitions: A contest in which two teams pull at opposite ends of a rope until one drags the other over a central line.
A struggle for supremacy or control between two opposing parties.
examples: The children played a tug-of-war at the picnic.
A political tug-of-war over the new budget.
